regimeSub: BOK delivers 25bp hike to 2.75% overnight; EM tightening cycle resumes despite soft US CPI print.
themes: BOK Governor Rhee hikes 25bp to 2.75% first time in 3.5 years → USDKRW tightens to 1485 from 1490 as won finds bid, but remains 3% off June highs
June CPI 3.5% vs 3.8% expected now fully digested → SPX +0.4% to 7572 as July FOMC hike odds remain suppressed despite Warsh's House testimony inflation warnings Monday
OPEC+ August quota expansion collides with renewed Hormuz hostilities per CNBC → Brent flat at $85.29, ECB reconsidering rate path on 'extremely volatile' energy outlook
SK Hynix HBM contract ceiling thesis confirmed → KOSPI rebounds 7% to 6753 from Monday crash but still 26% below June 22 ATH of 9114

signalNotes.name: US Real Rate
signalNotes.note: 10y real ~1.08% (4.58 nominal - 3.5% CPI); real rates positive but compressing on disinflation

signalNotes.name: 2s10s Curve
signalNotes.note: 2s10s at +40bp (4.58-4.18); curve steepening as front-end anchored, long end reflects fiscal/growth

signalNotes.name: HY Credit Spread
signalNotes.note: Spreads contained with VIX at 15.67; risk appetite stable despite BOK tightening signal

signalNotes.name: VIX
signalNotes.note: VIX 15.67 complacent; equity vol compressed despite EM rate hike cycle resuming

signalNotes.name: Brent Crude
signalNotes.note: Brent $85.29 rangebound; OPEC+ supply hike vs Hormuz risk = stalemate at pre-war levels

signalNotes.name: Gold
signalNotes.note: Gold $4037 holding post-June selloff lows; haven bid muted as real rates stay positive

signalNotes.name: USD / KRW
signalNotes.note: USDKRW 1485 tightens 5 won post-BOK hike; won still weak vs June levels despite tightening

signalNotes.name: Semiconductor SOX
signalNotes.note: KOSPI 6753 rebounds from HBM crash; SK Hynix contract ceiling now priced, watching Samsung

headline: BOK's first hike in 3.5 years marks EM tightening resumption as US disinflation (CPI 3.5% vs 3.8% exp) keeps Fed on hold, while Hormuz flare-ups freeze ECB rate path.
